description_long: This object consists of two adjacent apartment buildings with classic European architectural features. Both buildings have steep, dark gray gabled roofs with prominent chimneys and dormer windows. The facades are painted a light gray or off-white, with large, symmetrically arranged windows framed in dark trim. Each building has a single wooden entrance door at street level, accessed by a small step. The side and rear walls are plain, lacking windows or decorative elements, emphasizing the front-facing orientation. The overall design suggests a historical or traditional urban residential style, likely found in older European city neighborhoods.
description: Two adjacent, classic European-style apartment buildings with steep gray roofs, symmetrical window arrangements, and single wooden entrance doors.
description_view_0: Front view showing both buildings' facades, with large windows, wooden doors, and steep gabled roofs.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the plain, windowless side walls and the alignment of the two buildings' roofs and chimneys.
description_view_2: Rear view revealing the back facades, which are simpler but still feature some windows and the same roof style.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the plain, windowless side walls and the roof structure.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are symmetrically arranged in vertical columns on the front facades, with even spacing between floors and between the two buildings. Each floor has a consistent number of windows, contributing to a balanced and orderly appearance.
